title: marathrum squamosum var. phellandriifolium
intro: Marathrum squamosum var. phellandriifolium, also known as the Phellandrium-leaved Marathrum, is a plant species that belongs to the family Haloragaceae. It is a type of aquatic plant native to South America, particularly in the tropical regions of Brazil and Peru. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, and is believed to have health benefits such as reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating skin conditions. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, or infusions, and is often used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, although its effectiveness and safety have not been extensively scientifically studied.
